mission_anchor: Fully implement the single-daemon lifecycle upgrade for pi-code-index so it uses a persistent repo registry, supports automatic idle shutdown, unloads disabled repo runtimes immediately, and exposes daemon-wide observability through doctor/status flows without introducing a new public command surface.
plan_basis: Repo truth plus AGENTS.md and docs/mvp-spec.md Phase 1 constraints: keep one user-scoped Unix-socket daemon with a thin extension and no new public commands. Current implementation has per-process in-memory repo runtimes keyed by openRepo, SQLite baseline/overlay stores only, daemon wakeup for before_agent_start status gating, repo-local doctor/status only, and no persistent registry or idle shutdown lifecycle.

candidate_slices.goal: Add a durable daemon-owned repo registry and refactor lifecycle plumbing so persistent repo registration is the source of truth while loaded runtimes remain an in-memory cache layered on top.
acceptance_criteria: A durable registry stored under the existing user cache tracks repo identity, enabled state, and lifecycle metadata independently of baseline/overlay index stores, and survives daemon restart.

Daemon lifecycle code distinguishes registered repos from loaded runtimes so open/status/enable can materialize runtime state from registry truth instead of defaulting every unseen repo to disabled in memory.
On daemon startup or first repo access, enabled repos can be rediscovered from registry state with sane readiness metadata without introducing per-repo daemons or new public commands.
Deterministic tests cover registry persistence across daemon restart and truthful enabled-vs-loaded state restoration behavior.
candidate_slices.why_now: Persistent registry truth is the prerequisite for no-wakeup extension gating, immediate unload semantics that preserve cache, restart restoration, and authoritative daemon-wide observability.
evidence: review:completion-reviewer accepted HEAD a02764b17a4c719e538508bfb5429b02fc461d1f with no findings
locked_notes: Keep one user-scoped daemon singleton on a Unix domain socket.
Keep lifecycle authority in the daemon; extension remains thin.
Do not add new public commands, per-repo daemons, or cache purge-on-disable behavior.
implementation_surfaces: src/daemon/sqlite-store.ts or new daemon registry module

candidate_slices.goal: Use registry-backed truth to unload disabled repo runtimes immediately, perform daemon-wide idle detection with graceful auto-shutdown, and avoid unnecessary daemon wakeups by consulting registry hints first in extension startup/tool gating paths.
acceptance_criteria: `/index disable` flips persistent enabled state, stops queued/background work for that repo, unloads its runtime immediately, and preserves on-disk baseline/overlay/cache data.

When no repos remain enabled and there are no active requests or jobs, the daemon enters an idle grace period and then exits cleanly, removing socket and pid artifacts without launchd/systemd helpers.

Extension before_agent_start and any status/tool gating path that only needs enabled/disabled hints can consult durable registry data first so disabled or never-enabled repos do not wake the daemon unnecessarily.
Deterministic tests cover disable-triggered unload, idle auto-shutdown, clean restart after shutdown, and no-wakeup behavior for disabled or never-enabled repos where registry hints are sufficient.
candidate_slices.why_now: These behaviors depend on slice 1 making persistent registration authoritative and separating loaded runtimes from registry state.
locked_notes: Disable must preserve cache data by default.
Do not expand beyond macOS/Linux POSIX local transport assumptions.
implementation_surfaces: src/daemon/sqlite-store.ts or new daemon registry module

candidate_slices.goal: Expose authoritative daemon-wide lifecycle and registry observability through the existing doctor/status flows and lock in coverage with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Existing `/index doctor` output reports daemon-wide lifecycle state including enabled repo count, loaded runtime count, active jobs/requests, idle-shutdown eligibility or timer state, and registry summary information, while preserving current command surface.
`/index status` and related shared protocol/types remain truthful about repo enabled state versus loaded runtime state after restart, disable, and idle shutdown transitions.
Shared protocol and formatting changes remain Phase 1 compliant and deterministic, with no new query tools or semantic-search features introduced.
Deterministic tests verify daemon-wide observability fields and keep existing command/tool behavior passing.
candidate_slices.why_now: Authoritative observability should land after registry truth and idle lifecycle behavior exist so diagnostics reflect final daemon behavior instead of placeholders.
locked_notes: Use existing `/index status` and `/index doctor` commands only.
Keep outputs deterministic and Phase 1 scoped.
Do not add RFC-only tools or search surfaces.
